message:
chore(beads,docs): defer V1 markers and update FEATURE_PARITY

Open backlog cleanup driven from the deferred-subsystem policy in
FEATURE_PARITY.md (line 227): markers must remain visible, but they
are not actionable open work.

Beads:
- frankentorch-c3d (CUDA/GPU)        open -> deferred
- frankentorch-82b (DDP/FSDP)        open -> deferred
- frankentorch-8zy (TorchScript/JIT) open -> deferred
- frankentorch-iey (ONNX export)     open -> deferred
- frankentorch-dbp (upstream beads_rust import-path) open -> deferred
- frankentorch-hpg (QInt8/QUInt8 + QuantizedLinear)  open -> deferred

Newly filed:
- frankentorch-mbl (P3 bug) — flaky
  ft-conformance::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam,
  reproducibly fails on a clean main checkout
  (-4.7796 vs target -5, |delta|=0.22 vs 0.2 threshold). Pre-existing,
  unrelated to recent work; tracked for a follow-up agent.

FEATURE_PARITY.md:
- Quantization: not_started -> parity_partial (4ak MVP shipped, hpg
  follow-up tracked).
- Sparse tensors: not_started -> parity_partial (0tw MVP shipped).
- Mixed precision: not_started -> parity_partial (iha shipped).
- Gradient checkpointing: not_started -> parity_partial (plj shipped).
- ONNX, CUDA, JIT, distributed remain not_started with closure beads
  now in deferred state.

Result: 1 open bead (mbl, follow-up filed per session policy);
6 deferred markers; 2 beads shipped end-to-end this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"frankentorch-82b","title":"Add distributed training support (DDP/FSDP equivalents)","description":"FrankenTorch currently has no distributed-process orchestration, gradient synchronization, sharded parameter management, collective communication surface, or optimizer/state partitioning comparable to PyTorch DDP/FSDP.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Define distributed process-group / collective abstractions
2. Add replicated gradient synchronization for DDP-style training
3. Add parameter/state sharding for FSDP-style execution
4. Preserve deterministic replay and audit evidence across multi-rank execution
5. Add conformance scenarios for cross-rank gradient equivalence and failure handling

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can explicitly mark distributed training as deferred rather than implying it is already covered by the current CPU eager-mode slice.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:55.689135222Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:55.689135222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-8u7","title":"Make with_no_grad and with_enable_grad actually panic-safe","description":" and  are documented as panic-safe RAII equivalents, but they currently leave grad-enabled stack state dirty if the closure unwinds before the matching exit call. Restore state on panic and add regression coverage in ft-api.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:28.552416242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:09.461012260Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:09.460760282Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: with_no_grad/with_enable_grad use catch_unwind with proper cleanup. Two panic-safety regression tests already exist and pass (with_no_grad_restores_state_after_panic, with_enable_grad_restores_outer_no_grad_after_panic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-8zy","title":"Add TorchScript/JIT and torch.compile-style graph execution","description":"FrankenTorch currently executes an eager CPU-first tensor/autograd stack. It does not expose graph capture, TorchScript serialization/execution, ahead-of-time lowering, or `torch.compile`-style graph optimization/runtime selection.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Define graph capture / IR boundaries for eager programs
2. Add TorchScript-like serialization or a compatible alternative representation
3. Add compile-time graph lowering / optimization pipeline for `torch.compile`-style workflows
4. Preserve observable tensor semantics and DAC evidence under compiled execution
5. Add conformance scenarios comparing eager vs compiled outputs and gradients

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can explicitly mark JIT / `torch.compile` work as deferred instead of silently omitting a major PyTorch subsystem.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:24:01.515017019Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:24:01.515017019Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

4. Preserve observable tensor semantics and DAC evidence under compiled execution
5. Add conformance scenarios comparing eager vs compiled outputs and gradients

 {"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:33:49.271814166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c7c","title":"Add end-to-end training example and integration test","description":"FrankenTorch has e2e training loop tests buried in ft-optim's test suite, but there is no standalone, documented example showing users how to train a model. This is critical for proving the system works end-to-end and for user onboarding.

Create a comprehensive integration test (in ft-conformance or a new examples/ directory) that:

1. Defines a 2-layer MLP: Linear(784, 128) -> ReLU -> Linear(128, 10)
2. Generates synthetic MNIST-like data using the existing TensorDataset/DataLoader
3. Trains for 5 epochs using Adam optimizer with learning rate scheduling (StepLR or CosineAnnealing)
4. Tracks loss per epoch and asserts it decreases monotonically (with tolerance for noise)
5. Uses no_grad context for evaluation pass
6. Saves model state_dict to file, loads it back, verifies predictions match
7. Uses BatchNorm or LayerNorm in the architecture to exercise normalization paths
8. Logs: epoch number, batch count, loss per epoch, final accuracy estimate, elapsed time

This test exercises the full stack: ft-data (DataLoader) -> ft-nn (modules) -> ft-api (forward/backward) -> ft-autograd (gradient computation) -> ft-optim (parameter update) -> ft-serialize (save/load).

If any component fails to compose correctly, this test catches it.

Tests:
- The example IS the test: it should be a #[test] fn in ft-conformance or similar
- Assert loss decreases over 5 epochs
- Assert save/load round-trip produces identical predictions
- Assert no_grad eval context doesn't corrupt gradient state
- Log detailed per-epoch metrics for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:01.270413750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.299054985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.298796636Z","close_reason":"Added 5 E2E integration tests exercising the full stack: MLP regression with DataLoader/Adam/StepLR, binary classification with cross-entropy, save/load round-trip verification, no_grad eval safety, dataloader epoch shuffle. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-hpg","title":"Quantized storage dtypes (QInt8/QUInt8) and QuantizedLinear","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-4ak. The MVP fake-quantization slice landed in 4f6fff6 covers post-training calibration + QAT-style fake quantize using composed round/clamp ops, which keeps storage in the input dtype.

Remaining V1 work to fully match torch.quantization:
1. Add QInt8 / QUInt8 storage dtypes in ft-core (TensorStorage variant + DType enum + serde paths).
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta carrying scale + zero_point as part of TensorMeta.
3. Add a QuantizedLinear module in ft-nn that stores INT8 weights and dequantizes on the fly (or invokes a fused INT8 matmul kernel when added).
4. Per-channel quantization (per-axis scale/zero_point vectors).
5. Conformance fixtures comparing against torch.quantization output on a couple of canonical layers.

Out of scope here: GPU INT8 kernels (tracked under frankentorch-c3d).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:59.714118325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:59.714118325Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-iey","title":"Add ONNX model export support","description":"ONNX export allows FrankenTorch models to be deployed in any ONNX-compatible runtime (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, CoreML, etc.). This is important for production deployment and interoperability.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a new crate ft-onnx (or module in ft-serialize) that:
   a. Traces a model forward pass to capture the computation graph
   b. Maps FrankenTorch ops to ONNX operator set (opset 17+)
   c. Serializes the graph + weights to ONNX protobuf format
2. Add session.export_onnx(model, dummy_input, path) to ft-api
3. Map core ops: Linear, Conv2d, ReLU, BatchNorm, LSTM, Softmax, MatMul, Add, etc.
4. Use the onnx-pb crate for protobuf serialization (or manual protobuf encoding)

Tests:
- Unit test: export a Linear model, verify ONNX file is valid protobuf
- Unit test: exported model contains correct op types and shapes
- Integration test (if onnxruntime-rs available): run exported model and compare outputs
- Edge case: export model with dynamic batch size","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:10.592448782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990921829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-iey","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-2er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990453482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankentorch-igu","title":"Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
Success criterion: cargo test --workspace --all-features passes with all new property tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:58.165045573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092380322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092126241Z","close_reason":"Added 8 property-based tests: 4 in ft-autograd (add/mul gradient correctness, backward determinism, relu gradient indicator) + 4 in ft-kernel-cpu (neg self-inverse, exp/log round-trip, sum vs naive, softmax sums-to-one). All pass with proptest 256+ cases. Added proptest dev-dependency to ft-kernel-cpu.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iha","title":"Add mixed precision support (torch.amp equivalent)","description":"torch.cuda.amp (Automatic Mixed Precision) is used in virtually every modern training pipeline. Even on CPU, F16/BF16 mixed precision can be useful for inference and for testing AMP workflows before moving to GPU.

Since FrankenTorch already supports F16 and BF16 dtypes in ft-core, the infrastructure exists — what's missing is the autocast and grad scaling machinery.

Implementation plan:
1. Add an AutocastContext to ft-api that tracks whether autocast is active and the target dtype
2. Add session.autocast_enter(dtype) / session.autocast_exit() (matching no_grad pattern)
3. Add session.with_autocast(dtype, closure) panic-safe wrapper
4. When autocast is active: matmul, linear, conv operations auto-downcast inputs to the target dtype (F16/BF16), then upcast output back to F32
5. Add a GradScaler struct in ft-optim that:
   a. Scales loss by a factor before backward (prevents F16 underflow)
   b. Unscales gradients before optimizer step
   c. Checks for inf/nan in gradients, skipping the update if found
   d. Adjusts scale factor dynamically based on overflow frequency

Key: The autocast policy (which ops to cast) should match PyTorch's known-safe list — matmul, linear, conv are safe; softmax, layernorm, loss functions should stay in F32.

Tests:
- Unit test: autocast context enters/exits correctly, respects nesting
- Unit test: matmul inside autocast uses F16, outside uses F32
- Unit test: GradScaler scales loss and unscales gradients
- Unit test: GradScaler skips update on inf gradients
- E2E test: train a linear model with autocast + GradScaler, verify convergence
- Panic-safety: with_autocast restores state on unwind","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:09.970716552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450624484Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T23:42:34.450297625Z","close_reason":"Implemented mixed precision support: (1) ft-api: autocast_enter/exit/with_autocast/maybe_autocast with stack-based RAII context, panic-safe; (2) ft-optim: GradScaler with scale_loss/step/update + dynamic backoff/growth, overflow detection, skip-on-inf semantics; (3) ft-autograd: TensorBackwardReport::tensor_gradients_iter() and scaled_clone() helpers. 8 autocast tests + 7 GradScaler tests = 15 new tests. Note: F16/BF16 conversion paths gated until ft-autograd to_dtype supports them; F32/F64 down-casting works.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankentorch-mbl","title":"Flaky ft-conformance::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam (~0.22 vs 0.2 threshold)","description":"ft-conformance::tests::custom_function_layer_trains_end_to_end_with_adam reproducibly fails on a clean main checkout (verified at HEAD=fcad235 prior to 2026-04-27 work, and again at HEAD=dd9ae7b after).

Failure mode: 'prediction -4.779605595282768 should stay close to target -5' — the |pred - target| < 0.2 threshold at crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs:12780 fails by ~0.02 on a single element. The test trains 80 steps with Adam through a custom autograd function layer and asserts MSE-style convergence.

Reproducibility: deterministic — same numeric value on every run on the rch fleet. Suggests stochastic init not properly seeded OR the convergence threshold is just slightly too tight for the configured Adam lr/iter budget.

Suggested fix paths (any one is fine):
1. Seed the weight init explicitly via session.set_seed(...) and tune the threshold to actual convergence.
2. Increase iteration budget from 80 to 200 if accuracy matters.
3. Loosen the per-element threshold from 0.2 to 0.25 with a comment explaining the bound.

Not blocking shipped work — all touched paths still pass; this is a pre-existing test-suite stability issue surfaced during the 2026-04-27 reality check.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:31.545542241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:31.545542241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 ## Current Green Scope (CPU Eager-Mode Slice)
